sources: MLA (Modern Language Association) Job Market Report
Chronicle of Higher Education Academic Job Market Data
Versatile PhD and Beyond the Professoriate
Publishing and Editorial Industry Hiring Trends

smart.label: Research and Writing Rigor
what_counts: Humanities major (English, History, Philosophy, Languages, etc.)
Honors thesis, capstone, or independent research
Languages, archival work, or primary source analysis

grit.label: Quantifiable Impact and Ownership
what_counts: Research projects with clear scope and outcome
Teaching, tutoring, or mentoring with hours and impact
Editorial or publishing roles with deliverables
Events organized, readers served, or programs led
Sustained commitment to a cause or organization
Leadership in humanities orgs (literary journal, history club)

build.label: Evidence of Analysis and Output
what_counts: Writing samples: thesis, articles, essays with context
Editorial work: literary journal, newspaper, magazine
Teaching or tutoring with subject and outcomes
Research assistantships with PI and project named

common_gaps.gap: No publication, presentation, or substantial writing sample
common_gaps.fix: Submit to undergraduate journals, present at campus conferences, or publish in student media. Even one accepted piece or presentation is a baseline.

common_gaps.gap: Resume lacks evidence of analytical or research work
common_gaps.fix: Name your thesis topic, research methods, or primary sources. List relevant coursework. Show you can do sustained, rigorous analysis.

common_gaps.gap: No teaching, tutoring, or editorial experience
common_gaps.fix: Tutor through your university, join a literary journal, or volunteer as a writing consultant. Humanities employers value communication and pedagogy.
Specify: research on what, with whom, what methods, what output. 'Researched X under Prof. Y; produced 20-page analysis' beats 'conducted research.'
Add numbers: readers served, events organized, hours taught, words edited, students tutored. Humanities can be quantified more than many assume.
